report_name: EU Commission Hacked via Compromised Trivy Scanner in Major Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat group that emerged in late 2025, credited with orchestrating a supply chain attack against the European Commission by compromising the Trivy vulnerability scanner to steal an AWS API key and exfiltrate 92GB of data.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial gain via sale on dark web forums.

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious data broker who advertised the 91.7 GB of data stolen from the European Commission for sale on a dark web forum, indicating a collaboration with TeamPCP.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The attackers compromised the Trivy open-source security scanner, a trusted tool, to infiltrate the European Commission's network.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The malicious version of the Trivy scanner was used to find and steal a secret AWS API key from within the European Commission's environment.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The stolen AWS API key was used to gain administrative access to the European Commission's AWS account.

attack_patterns.name: Additional Cloud Credentials
attack_patterns.description: To maintain their foothold and establish persistence, the attackers created a new access key for an existing user within the compromised AWS account.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Once inside the AWS environment, the attackers performed reconnaissance to identify valuable data and systems, such as the infrastructure for the Europa.eu web platform.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used their privileged access to exfiltrate 91.7 GB of compressed data from the Commission's AWS storage services.

malware.name: Compromised Trivy Scanner
malware.description: A malicious version of the legitimate Trivy open-source vulnerability scanner, modified by TeamPCP to locate and exfiltrate an AWS API key.

tools.name: Trivy
tools.description: A popular open-source vulnerability scanner that was compromised in a supply chain attack to gain initial access to the European Commission's network.

tools.name: Amazon Web Services
tools.description: The cloud platform used by the European Commission. Attackers gained management rights to the AWS account using a stolen API key to discover infrastructure and exfiltrate data.

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: The primary victim of the data breach. The attackers compromised their AWS environment via a malicious Trivy scanner and exfiltrated 92GB of data.

identities.name: Affected EU Entities and Clients
identities.description: A group of 29 other EU entities and 42 internal clients who utilized the compromised Europa.eu platform and were potentially affected by the data breach.

infrastructure.name: Dark Web Data Market
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum where the data broker ShinyHunters listed the data stolen from the European Commission for sale.

indicators.name: AWS CreateAccessKey by Automated Tool
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS CreateAccessKey by Automated Tool
id: 2a8b3c1d-f4e5-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the creation of a new AWS access key, particularly when initiated by a service account or an automated tool, which could indicate a persistence technique following a credential compromise.'
references:
  - https://www.therecord.media/cert-eu-attributes-major-data-breach-to-teampcp-hacking-group
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1098.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName: CreateAccessKey
    userIdentity.type: AssumedRole
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate automated processes that rotate or create access keys.
indicators.description: Monitors for the creation of new AWS access keys, a technique used by TeamPCP for persistence after gaining initial access.

indicators.name: Anomalous Egress from Security Scanner
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Egress from Security Scanner
id: 8b7c6d5e-4f3a-4b2c-9d8e-7f6a5b4c3d2e
status: experimental
description: 'Detects network connections from known security scanning tools (like Trivy) to external endpoints that are not approved vendor update sites. This could indicate data exfiltration by a compromised tool.'
references:
  - https://www.therecord.media/cert-eu-attributes-major-data-breach-to-teampcp-hacking-group
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1537
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/trivy'
    Initiated: true
  filter:
    DestinationIp:
      # Add legitimate Aqua Security / Trivy update IPs/domains here
      - '1.1.1.1'
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Scanners configured to send reports to a legitimate, but unlisted, external service.
indicators.description: Monitors for network traffic from security scanners like Trivy to external destinations, which could be a sign of data exfiltration by a compromised tool.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
